text: ECC cuts agent latency and token spend without touching your prompts.

evidence.excerpt: Cut agent latency and token spend without touching your prompts.

text: ECC is a profiler that instruments every agent hop (tool calls, model routes, token cost) with zero prompt changes.

evidence.excerpt: Instruments every agent hop — tool calls, model routes, token cost — with zero prompt changes.

text: ECC auto-routes each step to the cheapest model that still passes its eval.

evidence.excerpt: Auto-routes each step to the cheapest model that still passes its eval.
Layered provider config resolution: Provider/model chosen from explicit arg, then env vars, then saved .llm.env file, defaulting to claude.
Provider factory resolving type from arg, LLM_PROVIDER env, or saved .llm.env; maps ProviderType to class.

text: A regression gate blocks any change that makes an agent slower or more expensive.

evidence.excerpt: Regression gate blocks any change that makes an agent slower or more expensive.

text: ECC drops in beside existing harnesses with no rewrite and no lock-in (cross-harness portability).

evidence.excerpt: Drops in beside existing harnesses; no rewrite, no lock-in.
Cross-harness portability: Same repo ships across Codex, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, Zed, and Copilot harnesses.

text: ECC delivers sub-100ms agent routing in production.

evidence.excerpt: ECC delivers sub-100ms agent routing in production.
UserPromptSubmit runs on every single message - adds latency to every prompt.
In our 50-task benchmark, mgrep + Claude Code used ~2x fewer tokens than grep-based workflows — a token benchmark, not a sub-100ms routing-latency measurement.

text: ECC is priced per traced agent-run.

evidence.excerpt: Sits next to the tracing tools teams already pay for, priced per traced agent-run.
OSS is MIT-licensed with paid Pro tier: Repo is MIT-licensed forever; ECC Pro GitHub App ($19/seat/mo) funds private-repo hosting.

text: ECC harness instrumentation is shipped and open-sourced.

evidence.excerpt: Working harness instrumentation shipped and open-sourced.
OSS is MIT-licensed with paid Pro tier: Repo is MIT-licensed forever.
README.md: Marketing entry point for ECC, the harness-native agent operating system; lists stars, sponsors, guides, and version history across harnesses.
